title: aws-sm: put ignores field selector, silently corrupts JSON secrets
description: Trigger: AwsSmBackend constructed with ?field=password, caller calls put(new_password). put replaces the entire secret string with raw value bytes, destroying all other fields. The field member is read in get (via fetch) but completely ignored in put, with no comment acknowledging this. Fix: either return SecretError::Backend explaining field-scoped put is unsupported, or implement read-modify-write. Silently doing the wrong thing is the worst outcome.
